items.title: Valve officially reveals Steam Machine’s exorbitant price, pre-orders live this week
items.summary: The Steam Machine is finally arriving and very soon at that, but if you thought there might be a chance Valve found a way to get the price down at all, think again. Pre-orders are set to go live this week and the thing is going to cost you more than $1,000 if you’re even “lucky” enough to get one.

items.title: Windows 11 Pro is now just $10 for life (Up to $199 value)
items.summary: If you’re looking for a quick and painless way to give your PC an overhaul, why not try this $9.97 (reg. $199) Microsoft Windows 11 Pro Lifetime License? It offers faster performance to handle demanding workloads, enhanced security features, improved management tools, AI capabilities, and an updated visual design to help you stay as efficient as possible.

items.title: Valve Steam Machine review: small and quiet, runs PC games well on a TV, but nearly twice the price of a PS5 for PS5 performance and needs manual configuration (Sean Hollister/The Verge)
items.summary:
Sean Hollister / The Verge:
Valve Steam Machine review: small and quiet, runs PC games well on a TV, but nearly twice the price of a PS5 for PS5 performance and needs manual configuration  —  My first day with the Steam Machine was a mess.  Instead of enjoying a worry-free game console, I spent hours troubleshooting what felt like a finicky PC.

items.title: Valve's Steam Machine, its new living room Linux PC, will start at $1,049 for the 512GB base model without a controller, and will go on sale starting June 29 (Jay Peters/The Verge)
items.summary:
Jay Peters / The Verge:
Valve's Steam Machine, its new living room Linux PC, will start at $1,049 for the 512GB base model without a controller, and will go on sale starting June 29  —  You can register your interest starting today, and the first emails letting people buy one will go out on June 29th.
